narrative: Dear [Student's Name], imagine the admissions team looking at your application like piecing together a story about your journey to the bar exam, and unfortunately, the picture it painted led to a tough call on your law school spot. Your Year 3 grades placed you in the bottom decile at school—that stood out as the biggest signal, whispering doubts about sustaining strong performance under law's intense pressures. Right behind that was your LSAT score of 30, which fell short of the benchmarks that typically light the path to bar success for others. Your Year 1 decile of 1 echoed that early struggle, suggesting a pattern rather than a one-off hurdle. Coming from a lower-middle family income added another layer, as the model sees it often means fewer resources to buffer the grueling prep ahead. Your Asian background played a role too, tilting the story toward higher risk in this prediction. Even your undergraduate GPA of 3.1, while solid, couldn't fully counterbalance those pulls toward failure. Your choice to go full-time nudged it further in that direction, as the model associates it with divided focus. Your gender as male was one of the few bright spots, gently pulling toward passing, but it wasn't enough to shift the overall tide. With a 44% predicted chance of not passing the bar, the full combination just didn't add up to the confident "yes" they need for spots in their program. It's not the end of your story—this is just one lens, and with targeted steps on grades and test prep, you could rewrite it elsewhere. Hang in there; your determination can change the narrative.
